mentions.note: Stock was down on the day; Cramer says it's a good level to start buying and to add more if it falls further.

Mentioned as long-term winner in Magnificent 7 / Fang+ portfolio; facing multiple compression and investor rotation to storage plays but Cramer maintains conviction on mega-cap tech reflow.
Down 8 straight weeks; other side of storage shortage trade — needs to pay sky-high storage prices, margin pressure. Own it, don't trade it; if guides margins down, decline may not be over.

Own it, don't trade it. 8-week losing streak but up $7.37 today on memory margin commentary. Watch gross margins, iPhone 17 durability, Gemini partnership ROI, and Siri reboot progress Thursday. Dodges massive CapEx rivals are burning.
Great momentum but trades at one of highest P/E multiples in entire market. Too much danger from valuation; Cramer avoiding despite company quality.

Reports Thursday after 8-week losing streak. Memory chip cost pressure on margins key watch. iPhone 17 strong launch should show in quarter. Gemini AI partnership may net $20B+ annually. Only Mag 7 company avoiding massive CapEx — own it don't trade it.
iPhone 17 sales $85B vs. $79B consensus; robust top/bottom beat; own it, don't trade it; monitor memory price headwinds.

Terrific quarter with 22% iPhone growth, America strength, first China growth in ages; bearish chip-cost thesis overblown; Tim Cook unusually bullish suggesting margin confidence; supply-chain mastery, customer power, and carrier subsidies protect pricing; finished +$120 despite midday weakness.

Tremendous quarter with strong U.S. and shocking China recovery; iPhone revenue +22%; finished up $1.20 despite bearish sell-off on parts shortage fears; Cramer confident supply chain mastery and pricing power will overcome.
Making a move as bear narrative shifts; gave terrific guidance when reported; pessimists starting to tremble; market perception changing.
iPhone blowout, China staggeringly good, gross margins strong; avoided billions in capex via Google Gemini partnership—own, don't trade.
Core 'own it don't trade it' conviction holding alongside Nvidia; favorite stock that has been right; hold through volatility; concentrated core portfolio.

Eric's portfolio; own it don't trade it; consistent strong buy.
Never really declined; Cramer thinks stock ready to roll higher given market recovery and still-solid fundamentals.
Soared 3% on rumors of strong Chinese sales; beat-down Mag 7 name catching up on rebalancing.
Huge beneficiary of AI for users; CEO Tim Cook transitioning to chairman (John Turnus takes over Sept 1); fundamental strength intact despite leadership change.

Tim Cook legacy: 1,900% stock gain, 43% buybacks, zero compromise on quality or customer satisfaction. Rare consumer enterprise that achieved scale and loyalty. Successor must maintain customer-first culture.
Own it, don't trade it; Tim Cook handing reins to John Turnus (executive chairman Sept 1); enough momentum to last end of year; new phones, foldables, iPhone 17 line keep moving higher.
Earnings Thursday night; part of mega-cap tech cluster that has coincided with market peaks and subsequent selloffs.
Revenue +17%, guidance good; no controversy despite iPhone questions; own it don't trade it; camera excellence (17 Pro Max) shows manufacturing mastery; should have popped more post-earnings.
Best-in-class hardware with Gemini AI integration and smart Siri improvement; perfectionism in product launch sustains premium valuation.
Unique position: not raising capital for AI; piggybacking on Alphabet's investment instead. WWDC Monday will detail Siri upgrade. Don't chase here.

Market leader with strong WWDC announcements (AI integration with Google/Gemini making Siri smarter). Traders sold it wrongly. Apple will keep improving and is critical to market leadership. Deserves to keep winning.
Strong balance sheet, robust buyback program, and beloved products insulate from tech sector weakness.
Don't sell on weakness; liquidation is for SpaceX/Anthropic/OpenAI IPO capital, not fundamental deterioration; own, don't trade.
Raising phone prices $200 to pass on memory chip cost inflation from hyperscaler demand. Consumer electronics getting expensive; could become national political issue if trend continues.
